name: Cajun Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 15 ounces Can Red Kidney Beans, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the peppers and cut them in half. Remove the seeds and membranes.
2. Place the pepper boats into a 9 by 12-inch baking dish. Pour 2 cups of water into the dish, taking care not to get any inside the pepper cavities.
3. Cover the dish with microwave-safe plastic wrap and microwave the peppers on high until they are just crisp-tender, about 5 to 6 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a 12-inch nonstick skillet that has a lid over medium heat.
5. Peel and coarsely chop the onion, adding it to the skillet as you chop. Add the garlic and Cajun spice, raising the heat to medium-high.
6. Cut the sausage link into quarters, then cut each quarter into 1/4-inch slices. Add the sausage to the pan.
7. Add broth, tomatoes, and rice to the skillet, cover, and raise the heat to high to bring the broth to a boil.
8. Rinse and drain the beans, then add them to the skillet and cover the pan.
9. Continue boiling until the rice is tender, about 5 minutes. Uncover and continue boiling, stirring occasionally, for an additional minute to thicken the sauce.
10. To serve, remove the peppers from the dish with a slotted spoon, draining any water. Place a pepper half on each plate and fill the cavities with stuffing. Spoon the remaining stuffing around the outside of the peppers.
11. Serve immediately. Serves 6.
